why: AI can write a function — it cannot decide Kafka vs RabbitMQ for your throughput, or tell you when CAP theorem means you must sacrifice consistency. This is the senior engineer's core skill.

topics.title: CAP theorem, consistency models, and partitioning
topics.drill: Draw CAP triangle. Place MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Cassandra, Redis on it. Justify each placement.

topics.title: Message queues: Kafka vs RabbitMQ vs SQS — decision criteria
topics.drill: For each: a payments pipeline, a chat app, a log aggregator — pick a queue. Justify in 2 sentences each.

topics.title: Database selection: SQL vs NoSQL vs time-series — when to use each
topics.drill: Design the DB layer for: Twitter, a bank, an IoT sensor fleet. Different DB for each? Why?

topics.title: Caching: write-through, write-back, cache-aside, invalidation strategies
topics.drill: Implement cache-aside for one endpoint. Add TTL. Handle cache stampede. Write the pseudocode.

topics.title: Load balancing: L4 vs L7, algorithms, sticky sessions
topics.drill: Explain round-robin vs least-connections vs IP-hash. When does sticky session break horizontal scaling?

topics.title: Rate limiting patterns: token bucket, sliding window, fixed window
topics.drill: Implement sliding-window rate limiter in Redis pseudocode. Handle distributed scenario (2 app servers).

topics.title: Circuit breaker pattern: states, thresholds, half-open logic
topics.drill: Draw the state machine for a circuit breaker. Write the threshold logic in pseudocode.

topics.title: Design a URL shortener at 100M URLs/day scale
topics.drill: Full system design: API, ID generation (no collisions), redirect performance, analytics. 20 minutes, no notes.

topics.title: Design a notification system (push, email, SMS) at scale
topics.drill: Handle 10M notifications/day. Prioritization queues, retry logic, unsubscribe compliance. Draw the architecture.

topics.title: Idempotency: why it matters and how to implement it
topics.drill: Explain idempotency key pattern for a payment API. What happens on network retry without it?

why: You don't need a PhD in ML. But you must be able to: take an existing model, adapt it to a domain-specific task, evaluate it honestly, and deploy it. That's the real moat.

topics.title: Transfer learning: why it works and when to use it
topics.drill: Explain transfer learning to someone who knows linear algebra but no ML. Use one concrete image classification example.

topics.title: LoRA and QLoRA fine-tuning: the math and the workflow
topics.drill: Explain what LoRA does to the weight matrices in plain language. Why does it require less VRAM than full fine-tuning?

topics.title: HuggingFace basics: datasets, tokenizers, trainers
topics.drill: Walk through the full HuggingFace fine-tuning pipeline from scratch in pseudocode: load dataset → tokenize → train → evaluate → push to hub.

topics.title: Data preparation for fine-tuning: quality over quantity
topics.drill: For a customer support chatbot fine-tune: describe the ideal training data format, size, and quality criteria.

topics.title: Evaluation metrics for LLMs: BLEU, ROUGE, human eval, LLM-as-judge
topics.drill: Explain why perplexity is a poor proxy for real-world quality. What would you use instead for a coding assistant?

topics.title: RAG vs fine-tuning: decision framework
topics.drill: When do you RAG and when do you fine-tune? Write the decision tree with 5 criteria.

topics.title: Vector databases and embedding retrieval at scale
topics.drill: Compare ChromaDB vs Pinecone vs pgvector for: a startup with <100K docs, an enterprise with 10M docs.

topics.title: Prompt engineering: system prompts, few-shot, chain-of-thought
topics.drill: Rewrite a bad prompt for a code review assistant using: role + context + format + examples. Show the before/after.

resources.name: Andrej Karpathy — Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
resources.author: Andrej Karpathy

why: When a microservice fails silently in production at 2am, no LLM can diagnose it — only structured telemetry can. You need to build systems that tell you what's wrong before users do.

topics.title: The three pillars: metrics, logs, traces — differences and when to use each
topics.drill: A request is slow intermittently. Walk through: what metric catches it, what log gives context, what trace shows root cause.

topics.title: OpenTelemetry: instrumentation, context propagation, exporters
topics.drill: Instrument a Python function with OTel: add a span, baggage, and an attribute. Export to Jaeger. Pseudocode is fine.

topics.title: Prometheus: counters, gauges, histograms, summaries — when to use which
topics.drill: Design the metrics for an HTTP API: request rate, error rate, latency distribution. Write the Prometheus metric types for each.

topics.title: Grafana dashboards: the USE method and RED method
topics.drill: Build a USE method dashboard for a single service: Utilization, Saturation, Errors for CPU, memory, network, disk.

topics.title: Log aggregation: structured logging, ELK vs Loki, log levels
topics.drill: Write a structured log line for a failed payment. Include: request_id, user_id, amount, error_code, latency_ms.

topics.title: SLIs, SLOs, SLAs, and error budgets
topics.drill: Define SLIs for an API (availability + p99 latency). Set SLO targets. Calculate the monthly error budget in minutes.

topics.title: Alerting strategy: alert on symptoms, not causes
topics.drill: Explain why 'CPU > 80%' is a bad alert. Write a better alert for the same system using user-facing symptoms.

topics.title: Distributed tracing: spans, trace context, sampling strategies
topics.drill: Trace a request through: API Gateway → Auth Service → DB. Draw the span waterfall. Where is the bottleneck?

why: AI writes insecure code constantly and confidently. You need to be the one who catches it, fixes it, and architects systems that are secure by default — not by accident.

topics.title: OAuth2 / OIDC: authorization code flow, PKCE, implicit vs hybrid
topics.drill: Draw the full OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE flow. Label every request and token. Where is the attack surface?

topics.title: JWTs: structure, signing algorithms, validation pitfalls
topics.drill: Exploit: alg:none attack, weak secret brute-force, missing aud/iss validation. Fix all three.

topics.title: Encryption at rest and in transit: symmetric vs asymmetric, when to use each
topics.drill: Design the encryption strategy for: user passwords, DB columns with PII, API communication, S3 objects.

topics.title: Zero-trust architecture: principles and implementation patterns
topics.drill: Describe zero-trust for a microservices backend. What replaces the perimeter? How is every service-to-service call authenticated?

topics.title: OWASP Top 10 (2023) deep dive: injection, broken access control, misconfigurations
topics.drill: For each of the top 5 OWASP items: write one attack vector, one test, one fix.

topics.title: Secrets management: vault patterns, rotation, detection
topics.drill: Design a secrets lifecycle: how are secrets created, stored, rotated, and revoked? Use HashiCorp Vault concepts.

topics.title: Supply chain security: dependency scanning, SBOMs, typosquatting
topics.drill: Audit one real project's dependencies with pip-audit or npm audit. Document every vulnerability and its fix.

topics.title: Penetration testing methodology: recon, scanning, exploitation, reporting
topics.drill: Run OWASP ZAP against a local app. Write a 1-page report: finding, severity, evidence, remediation.

topics.title: Secure SDLC: threat modeling, security reviews, shift-left
topics.drill: Apply STRIDE threat modeling to one endpoint you've built: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Info disclosure, DoS, Elevation.

why: AI can generate Terraform but will happily spin up $10,000/month of infrastructure. You need to architect for cost, reliability, and scalability — and know when to say no to complexity.

topics.title: Terraform: state management, modules, workspaces, remote backends
topics.drill: Write Terraform for a VPC + EC2 + RDS. Use modules. Remote state in S3 + DynamoDB locking. Explain the state file.

topics.title: AWS cost optimization: reserved instances, savings plans, spot instances
topics.drill: A startup is spending $5K/month on AWS. Identify the top 3 cost reduction strategies without hurting availability.

topics.title: Serverless vs Containers: decision framework and cold start mitigation
topics.drill: Compare Lambda vs ECS Fargate vs EC2 for: a cron job, a real-time API, a batch image processor. Pick one for each.

topics.title: Autoscaling: horizontal vs vertical, predictive vs reactive, cooldown periods
topics.drill: Design autoscaling for an API that spikes 10x every morning at 9am. Prevent cold start penalty on the spike.

topics.title: IAM: principle of least privilege, roles vs users, permission boundaries
topics.drill: Audit one AWS IAM policy for overpermission. Rewrite it with least privilege. Explain every permission you removed.

topics.title: CDN and edge computing: cache-control, invalidation, edge functions
topics.drill: Design a CDN strategy for a global SaaS app: which assets to cache, cache-control headers, purge on deploy.

topics.title: Disaster recovery: RPO, RTO, backup strategies, multi-region
topics.drill: Define RPO and RTO for a payment system. Design the backup and failover strategy that meets both.

topics.title: FinOps: tagging strategy, cost allocation, chargeback models
topics.drill: Design an AWS tagging strategy for a 20-engineer company: how to attribute costs to teams, features, environments.

why: Data pipelines are the backbone of modern AI products. If you can design and build a reliable data infrastructure, you're indispensable on any team building with AI.

topics.title: Batch vs stream processing: trade-offs and when each breaks
topics.drill: Compare batch vs stream for: daily sales report, fraud detection, social feed. Pick the winner for each and explain why.

topics.title: Apache Kafka: topics, partitions, consumer groups, offset management
topics.drill: Design a Kafka topic for a ride-share app: partition strategy, retention policy, consumer group for pricing vs analytics.

topics.title: Apache Spark: RDDs vs DataFrames, partitioning, shuffle operations
topics.drill: Explain why a Spark job is slow. Debug: check for data skew, shuffle read/write, spill to disk. Write the fix.

topics.title: Airflow: DAGs, task dependencies, XComs, sensors, backfill
topics.drill: Design an Airflow DAG: extract 5 API sources → transform with Pandas → load to Postgres. Handle partial failures.

topics.title: Data warehouses: Snowflake vs BigQuery vs Redshift — architecture differences
topics.drill: When would you choose BigQuery over Snowflake? Write 3 decision criteria with one concrete example each.

topics.title: ETL vs ELT: when each pattern is correct and its failure modes
topics.drill: You have 50GB of raw logs to analyze. Design: ELT into BigQuery vs ETL with Spark. Which is cheaper and faster? Why?

topics.title: Data quality: Great Expectations, schema validation, data contracts
topics.drill: Write 5 Great Expectations tests for a user events table. Include: not_null, uniqueness, value ranges, referential integrity.

topics.title: Change Data Capture (CDC): Debezium, log-based vs trigger-based
topics.drill: Design a CDC pipeline from PostgreSQL to Kafka using Debezium. What happens if the connector goes down?

resources.name: Apache Spark with Python — Big Data (freeCodeCamp)
resources.author: freeCodeCamp

why: The engineers who 10x their team's output are the ones who automate the boring parts: CI/CD, code quality gates, developer tooling. You must build the infrastructure that makes everyone else faster.

topics.title: CI/CD: GitHub Actions full pipeline — lint, test, build, deploy
topics.drill: Write a GitHub Actions workflow: on PR → run tests → on merge to main → build Docker image → push → deploy to staging.

topics.title: Blue-green and canary deployments: implementation and rollback
topics.drill: Design a canary deployment for an API: route 5% traffic to new version, monitor error rate, auto-rollback threshold.

topics.title: Custom CLI tools: building developer experience improvements
topics.drill: Build a CLI tool in Python or Go that scaffolds a new microservice: creates folder structure, Dockerfile, CI config, README.

topics.title: Pre-commit hooks: code quality gates before code hits CI
topics.drill: Set up pre-commit with: black, ruff, mypy, bandit, detect-secrets. Configure to run on staged files only. Fast failure.

topics.title: Agentic orchestration: LLM agents with tool use and memory
topics.drill: Design an AI agent that: reads a GitHub issue, searches the codebase, proposes a fix, and opens a PR. What tools does it need?

topics.title: Code review automation: quality gates, linting, security scanning in CI
topics.drill: Design a CI quality gate pipeline: unit tests (80% coverage minimum), lint score, security scan (block on Critical CVEs), dead code detection.

topics.title: GitOps: ArgoCD, Flux — declarative infrastructure management
topics.drill: Explain the GitOps model: how does a git push trigger an infrastructure change? Draw the ArgoCD sync loop.

topics.title: Developer environment as code: devcontainers, dotfiles, bootstrapping
topics.drill: Write a devcontainer.json that bootstraps a Python dev environment: VS Code extensions, port forwards, post-create commands.
